seasonal_rhythms.spring: debts are renegotiated when the thaw market opens the road
seasonal_rhythms.summer: courtship leaves the porch and goes public by the river
seasonal_rhythms.autumn: harvest tables settle old scores under the cover of manners
seasonal_rhythms.winter: the cemetery and clinic share more traffic than anyone admits

local_scandals: a lost tithe box that was never truly explained
letters hidden behind the Blue Lantern shelves
the old suspicion that relief money fattened the wrong ledgers
long_running_feuds: the Vale-Harrow quarrel over the mill sluice
the Bell suspicion that the tavern keeps what the church cannot
unwritten_customs: No widow sits alone on Bell Night.
A family debt is not named aloud before harvest unless the speaker wants a feud.
If the midwife asks for silence, even enemies pretend to be discreet.

collective_memory.summary: The flood of 1887 rearranged debt more thoroughly than the riverbank.
The year the tithe box vanished, everyone learned how piety can sound like bookkeeping.

economic_conditions: credit loosens only when a festival makes everyone sentimental
political_shifts: Ruth Vale has held civic influence long enough for duty to resemble dynasty.

family_vale.name: Vale
family_vale.origin: Mill owners who turned flood relief into civic authority.
inherited_secrets: The flood ledger has never been fully opened.
inherited_loyalties: Protect the mill before pride, then pretend the order was reversed.

collective_memory.summary: The Vales survived the flood by turning favor into obligation.

chronicle_notes: 1887: the family emerged richer in leverage than in sympathy.

family_harrow.name: Harrow
family_harrow.origin: Working mill folk who remember every insult as labor already paid for.
family_harrow.motto: We keep count because no one else will.
inherited_secrets: Someone in the family has always adjusted the record when truth would destroy a woman first.
inherited_loyalties: Stand by kin even when kin makes the burden heavier.

collective_memory.summary: The Harrows never forgot who kept the sluice high when their wages were low.

chronicle_notes: 1890: the sluice grievance became family scripture.

family_quill.name: Quill
family_quill.origin: River people who trade in conversation, wakeful hours, and the price of burial.
family_quill.motto: If we must listen, we may as well remember.
inherited_secrets: The tavern shelves hold more confessions than the church register.
inherited_loyalties: Never embarrass your own blood in front of a paying room.

collective_memory.summary: The Quills learned that paper can weigh more than stone if the names are right.

chronicle_notes: 1898: Ada hid a packet and called it tidying.

family_bell.name: Bell
family_bell.origin: Clergy and caregivers whose respectability survives by keeping a careful distance from appetite.
inherited_secrets: Mercy's birth was explained on paper more neatly than in blood.
inherited_loyalties: Protect public respectability even when private truth goes hungry.

collective_memory.summary: After the tithe scandal, the Bells preached harder than before and trusted less.

chronicle_notes: 1894: Gideon survived suspicion by sounding more certain.

household_vale_manor.name: Vale Manor
assets: a carriage with a failing axle
the flood ledger locked in a cedar chest
notes: Dinner is where affection goes to be audited.

household_harrow_cottage.name: Harrow Cottage
assets: a sewing machine bought on credit
a drawer of receipts Jonah keeps like evidence
notes: Every quarrel is interrupted by useful work.

household_quill_rooms.name: Quill Rooms
assets: a violin Clara keeps hidden in a flour sack
notes: People pay for drink, burial, and listening in roughly that order.

household_bell_parsonage.name: Bell Parsonage
notes: Respectability is polished daily here, like silver that resents fingerprints.

char_ruth_vale.family_origin: Vale mill money and civic office
char_ruth_vale.private_voice: She counts affection the way other people count rent: carefully, because once it cost too much.
char_ruth_vale.public_voice: Measured, civic, and faintly maternal even when she is closing a door.
char_ruth_vale.poetic_voice: A bell of office rung from beneath old water.

memories.summary: She watched the flood ledger close and understood that gratitude can be arranged like a mortgage.

secrets.summary: She buried the proof that Silas profited from relief money.
secrets.truth: I kept the flood ledger because it showed Silas drawing relief funds into the mill while the Harrows pawned blankets.

reputation.public_summary: The town credits Ruth with keeping the mill standing and fears the cost of her favors.

char_silas_vale.family_origin: Vale mill money and civic office
char_silas_vale.private_voice: He jokes when frightened and bargains when ashamed.
char_silas_vale.public_voice: A tired patriarch who still expects the room to arrange itself around him.
char_silas_vale.poetic_voice: A mill wheel speaking after it has broken a hand too many.

memories.summary: He signed his name in a relief ledger and called theft stewardship because the mill had to survive somebody's hunger.

secrets.summary: He treated flood aid as private capital.
secrets.truth: I drew relief money into the mill and let gratitude stand in for wages because power was easier to keep than fairness.

reputation.public_summary: Silas still carries authority, though age has made him look more like evidence than leadership.

char_caleb_vale.family_origin: Vale mill money and civic office
char_caleb_vale.private_voice: He wishes for a life that sounds less inherited.
char_caleb_vale.public_voice: Polite, learned, and almost too careful to be trusted.
char_caleb_vale.poetic_voice: A classroom window left open toward the cemetery.

memories.summary: At Harvest Supper he watched Miriam Harrow laugh without permission and wanted a life that could follow that sound.

secrets.summary: He keeps Miriam's letters under a geography atlas.
secrets.truth: I kept Miriam Harrow's letters under the school atlas and called that arrangement restraint when it was only cowardice with stationery.

char_miriam_harrow.public_label: seen lingering by the school gate

reputation.public_summary: Caleb is praised for gentleness in a town that says the word as if it were a warning.

char_jonah_harrow.private_voice: He has been angry so long that tenderness sounds to him like bad accounting.
char_jonah_harrow.public_voice: Blunt, useful, and one insult away from a speech.
char_jonah_harrow.poetic_voice: A man shaped by sawdust, river damp, and the long memory of wages.

memories.summary: He stood in town hall while the Vales spoke of fairness and knew the word had been ruined for him.

secrets.summary: He once altered the sluice markers to embarrass the Vales.
secrets.truth: I shifted the sluice markers one wet spring to make the Vales look greedy, and hated them harder when the trick still felt righteous.

reputation.public_summary: Jonah Harrow is respected for endurance and avoided when endurance turns theatrical.

char_eliza_harrow.private_voice: She knows where the town has bled and where it lied about the wound.
char_eliza_harrow.public_voice: Practical, tender, and impossible to hurry.
char_eliza_harrow.poetic_voice: Lamplight over linen, speaking long after the births have stopped crying.

memories.summary: She changed one name in a birth ledger and felt the whole town tilt around that small mercy.

secrets.summary: She altered Mercy Bell's birth record.
secrets.truth: I changed Mercy Bell's father in the birth ledger because the living men would have survived the gossip and the dead woman would not.

reputation.public_summary: Eliza Harrow is the person the town trusts with blood but not entirely with silence.

char_miriam_harrow.private_voice: She is tired of being told dignity looks best in restraint.
char_miriam_harrow.public_voice: Quick, capable, and one joke too bright for respectable company.
char_miriam_harrow.poetic_voice: A hemline catching on a gravestone thorn and calling it destiny.

memories.summary: At Harvest Supper she let Caleb Vale walk her home and pretended the entire road was not a scandal already.

secrets.summary: She would leave town with Caleb if he chose courage.
secrets.truth: I said I wanted nothing from Caleb Vale that the town could name, and I would have boarded the first train with him if he had arrived at the station before dawn.

reputation.public_summary: Miriam Harrow is liked better in private than in committee, which she considers a compliment.

char_samuel_harrow.private_voice: He listens at doors because the truth always seems to begin in another room.
char_samuel_harrow.public_voice: Quiet until he is sure he has learned the useful part.
char_samuel_harrow.poetic_voice: A boy storing thunder in his pockets.

memories.summary: He heard Jonah say the word Vale like a curse and decided adults choose enemies the way children choose marbles.

reputation.public_summary: Samuel is treated as harmless, which is teaching him habits the adults may regret.

char_ada_quill.family_origin: Quill rooms above the Blue Lantern
char_ada_quill.private_voice: She jokes at the edge of pain because solemnity has never once paid the lease.
char_ada_quill.public_voice: Warm enough to attract confession and sharp enough to survive it.
char_ada_quill.poetic_voice: Counter wood, midnight glasses, and a candle still refusing the dawn.

memories.summary: She found Gideon Bell's letters behind a cracked shelf and understood that virtue leaves paper trails when desire becomes literate.

secrets.summary: She keeps Gideon Bell's old love letters hidden behind the bar.
secrets.truth: I kept Gideon Bell's letters to the singer from Galena behind the brandy shelf because holiness deserves at least one witness who can laugh.

reputation.public_summary: Ada Quill is welcomed by people who insist, later, that they never trusted her.

char_thomas_quill.family_origin: Quill rooms above the Blue Lantern
char_thomas_quill.private_voice: Death made him patient with bodies and impatient with vanity.
char_thomas_quill.public_voice: Reserved, careful, and unexpectedly courtly when grief enters the room.
char_thomas_quill.poetic_voice: Black wool, cedar shavings, and the soft authority of a man who closes lids.

memories.summary: He watched Mercy Bell wash a dead child with such steadiness that desire began in him as reverence.

secrets.summary: He has built a room he hopes Mercy Bell will one day use.
secrets.truth: I built a spare room over the workshop and measured it for Mercy Bell before I had even held her hand properly.

reputation.public_summary: Thomas Quill is the kind of man people call steady when they do not know how lonely they mean.

char_clara_quill.family_origin: Quill rooms above the Blue Lantern
char_clara_quill.private_voice: She hears escape in any tune played softly enough.
char_clara_quill.public_voice: Half dutiful, half already leaving in imagination.
char_clara_quill.poetic_voice: A fiddle string learning the cemetery wind.

memories.summary: She saw the packet of letters once and knew paper could frighten adults more than knives.

reputation.public_summary: Clara is young enough to be overlooked and old enough to notice the advantage.

char_gideon_bell.private_voice: He loves doctrine best when it drowns out memory.
char_gideon_bell.public_voice: Sonorous, composed, and offended by appetite in whatever body it occupies.
char_gideon_bell.poetic_voice: A cracked hymn book telling the truth between verses.

memories.summary: He nearly ran away with a singer from Galena and spent the next twenty years preaching against the temptation of itinerant charm.

secrets.summary: He wrote love letters he still calls a spiritual lapse.
secrets.truth: I wrote letters to a singer and nearly left the parish with her, then preached twice as hard because desire had learned my handwriting.

reputation.public_summary: Gideon Bell still sounds righteous enough to make people forget how often righteousness is a costume.

char_mercy_bell.private_voice: She confuses usefulness with deserving love and knows the confusion may ruin her.
char_mercy_bell.public_voice: Calm in crisis, distant when anyone asks how she is sleeping.
char_mercy_bell.poetic_voice: Bandage, winter lamp, and a pulse counted after everyone else stops listening.

memories.summary: Thomas Quill held a basin for her without making a performance of pity, and she has trusted the gesture longer than she trusts language.

secrets.summary: She suspects Silas Vale is her father.
secrets.truth: I have long suspected that Silas Vale, not any Bell, gave me my face, and I hated the suspicion because it felt too much like belonging to the wrong room.

reputation.public_summary: Mercy Bell is the person most people want nearby in a crisis and least understand afterward.
